title: senecio canescens var. monocephalus
intro: Senecio canescens var. monocephalus, also known as the single-headed woolly groundsel, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and laxative, due to its alkaloid content, specifically senecionine and senkirkine. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including stimulating digestion and relieving constipation, although its use is often cautioned against due to potential toxicity and side effects. In herbal preparations, Senecio canescens var. monocephalus is sometimes used in small quantities, often combined with other herbs, and is typically administered as a tea or tincture under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
